user_story: As a CMDB operator, I want a single summary of total CIs, breakdowns by type/status/environment, and recent discoveries so that I can gauge inventory health at a glance.
expected_behavior: Opens a Neo4j session and runs MATCH queries to count total CIs, unique types/environments, total relationships, and group CIs by type, status, and environment; also returns the last 10 CIs whose metadata contains 'discovery_provider' with Neo4j DateTime converted to ISO strings. Responds 200 with a flat object {total_cis, by_type, by_status, by_environment, recent_discoveries, health_score, critical_relationships} (note: not wrapped in the _success/_data envelope the sibling endpoints use). On error logs and returns 500; the Neo4j session is always closed in finally.
issue_note: health_score is hardcoded to 0 (analytics.controller.ts:579) and critical_relationships is set to the total relationship count, not critical ones (analytics.controller.ts:580) -- both are misleading placeholders.

user_story: As an asset manager, I want CI counts grouped by ci_type so that I can see the composition of my inventory.
expected_behavior: Queries Postgres dim_ci WHERE is_current = true, GROUP BY ci_type ORDER BY count DESC. Responds 200 with {_success:true, _data:[{ci_type, count}, ...]}. On query failure logs the error and returns 500 with {_success:false,_error,_message}. Takes no parameters.

user_story: As an operations lead, I want CI counts grouped by status so that I can see how many CIs are active, inactive, in maintenance, or decommissioned.
expected_behavior: Queries Postgres dim_ci WHERE is_current = true, GROUP BY ci_status (aliased status) ORDER BY count DESC. Responds 200 with {_success:true,_data:[{status,count},...]}. On failure returns 500 with the standard error envelope. No parameters.

user_story: As a platform owner, I want CI counts grouped by environment so that I understand the dev/test/prod distribution of my estate.
expected_behavior: Queries Postgres dim_ci WHERE is_current = true AND environment IS NOT NULL, GROUP BY environment ORDER BY count DESC. Responds 200 with {_success:true,_data:[{environment,count},...]}; rows with null environment are excluded. On failure returns 500. No parameters.

user_story: As an architect, I want relationship counts grouped by relationship_type so that I can understand how CIs are interconnected.
expected_behavior: Queries Postgres cmdb.fact_ci_relationships, GROUP BY relationship_type ORDER BY count DESC. Responds 200 with {_success:true,_data:[{relationship_type,count},...]}. On failure returns 500 with the error envelope. No parameters.

user_story: As a discovery administrator, I want summary discovery stats over a date range and a breakdown by provider so that I can measure discovery coverage.
expected_behavior: Builds an optional discovered_at >= / <= filter from start_date/end_date query params and runs two Postgres queries on dim_ci (is_current=true): a summary (total_cis, unique_types, first/last discovery) and per-provider counts. Responds 200 with {_success:true,_data:{_summary,_by_provider},_filters:{start_date,end_date}}. On failure returns 500.
issue_note: Route schema dateRangeSchema keys on _start_date/_end_date (analytics.routes.ts:14-17) but the controller reads start_date/end_date (analytics.controller.ts:137); validate() runs stripUnknown:true (validators.ts:36) and validateOptional overwrites req.query (validation.middleware.ts:153), so the supplied dates are dropped and the date filter never applies.

user_story: As an analyst, I want a time-bucketed timeline of CI discovery counts so that I can visualize discovery activity over time.
expected_behavior: Validates interval against ['hour','day','week','month'] (controller returns 400 for invalid), caps limit at 365, then runs a Postgres query using date_trunc($interval, discovered_at) over dim_ci (is_current=true), GROUP BY period ORDER BY period DESC LIMIT $limit. Responds 200 with {_success:true,_data:[{period,count,unique_types}],interval}. On failure returns 500.
issue_note: Route timelineSchema keys on _interval/_limit (analytics.routes.ts:19-22) while the controller reads interval/limit (analytics.controller.ts:205); stripUnknown:true + req.query overwrite drop the user values, so interval/limit always fall back to defaults (day/30) regardless of input.

user_story: As an architect, I want the most-connected CIs ranked by relationship count and filterable by direction so that I can find critical hubs in the topology.
expected_behavior: Reads limit (capped at 100) and direction; for direction 'in' joins fact_ci_relationships on to_ci_id, 'out' on from_ci_id, otherwise (both) LEFT JOINs both and sums distinct counts. Responds 200 with {_success:true,_data:[{ci_id,ci_name,ci_type,relationship_count}],direction}. On failure returns 500.
issue_note: Route topConnectedSchema keys on _limit/_direction (analytics.routes.ts:24-27) but the controller reads limit/direction (analytics.controller.ts:256); stripUnknown:true + req.query overwrite discard the user values, so the query always uses default direction 'both' and limit 10.

user_story: As an architect, I want dependency depth statistics so that I can identify deeply nested DEPENDS_ON chains and the overall depth distribution.
expected_behavior: Runs two recursive CTEs over cmdb.fact_ci_relationships filtered to relationship_type='DEPENDS_ON' (capped at depth<10): the first returns the top 100 CIs by max_depth with total_dependencies, the second returns the count of CIs per max_depth. Responds 200 with {_success:true,_data:{_top_cis,_depth_distribution}}. On failure returns 500. No parameters.

user_story: As an auditor, I want the change history for a specific CI so that I can review what fields changed, when, and by whom.
expected_behavior: Requires ci_id (controller returns 400 'CI ID is required' when missing), caps limit at 1000, then queries ci_change_history WHERE ci_id=$1 ORDER BY change_timestamp DESC LIMIT $2. Responds 200 with {_success:true,_data:[{change_timestamp,change_type,field_name,old_value,new_value,changed_by}],ci_id}. On failure returns 500.
issue_note: Route changeHistorySchema declares _ci_id as required (analytics.routes.ts:29-32) but clients/controller use ci_id (analytics.controller.ts:415); since validateOptional runs whenever any query key is present, a request with ?ci_id=X fails Joi (missing required _ci_id) and returns 400 Validation Error, making the endpoint effectively unusable.

user_story: As an SRE, I want recent anomalies within a time window so that I can triage configuration anomalies as they appear.
expected_behavior: Parses hours (default 24, route caps 1-168) and limit (default 100, route caps 1-1000) and delegates to anomalyEngine.getRecentAnomalies(hours, limit). Responds 200 with {_success:true,_data:[Anomaly,...]}. On engine error logs and returns 500. Route uses plain (non-underscore) schema keys matching the controller.

user_story: As an operator, I want all anomalies for a specific CI so that I can investigate a problematic component.
expected_behavior: Reads :ciId path param (defaults to empty string if absent) and limit (default 50, route caps 1-500), then calls anomalyEngine.getAnomaliesForCI(ciId, limit). Responds 200 with {_success:true,_data:[Anomaly,...]}. On error logs and returns 500.

user_story: As a manager, I want aggregate anomaly counts by severity, type, and status so that I can understand the anomaly landscape.
expected_behavior: Runs four Postgres queries against the anomalies table: total count, GROUP BY severity, GROUP BY anomaly_type (ordered by count), and GROUP BY status; assembles them into maps. Responds 200 with {_success:true,_data:{total,by_severity,by_type,by_status}}. On failure returns 500. No parameters.

user_story: As an analyst, I want to update an anomaly's status (and record who resolved it) so that I can track triage and resolution.
expected_behavior: Validates status against ['investigating','resolved','false_positive','confirmed','ignored'] (controller returns 400 if invalid). Runs an UPDATE on anomalies setting status, and conditionally setting resolved_at=NOW() and resolved_by when status is 'resolved' or 'false_positive', RETURNING *. Returns 404 if no row matched, otherwise 200 with {_success:true,_data:mappedAnomaly}. On failure returns 500.

user_story: As an operator, I want to manually trigger anomaly detection so that I can refresh anomaly results without waiting for the scheduled run.
expected_behavior: Logs the manual trigger and calls anomalyEngine.detectAnomalies(). Responds 200 with {_success:true,_data:{detected_count, anomalies}} where detected_count is anomalies.length. On engine failure logs and returns 500. Side effect: runs the detection engine which may persist newly detected anomalies. No request body required.

user_story: As an integration engineer, I want to find an existing CI that matches a set of identifiers so that I can avoid creating duplicates.
expected_behavior: Validates body via matchRequestSchema (identifiers object required) and re-checks identifiers is an object (400 otherwise). Maps identifiers into IdentificationAttributes, builds a minimal TransformedCI context, and calls reconciliationEngine.findExistingCI. If no match, responds 200 with {_success:true,_data:null,_message:'No matching CI found'}; if matched, 200 with {_success:true,_data:{ci_id,confidence,match_strategy,matched_attributes}}. On failure returns 500. Audit middleware applies to all reconciliation routes.

user_story: As a discovery pipeline, I want to merge a discovered CI into the CMDB so that its data is reconciled into the canonical record.
expected_behavior: Validates body via mergeRequestSchema (name, ci_type, source, source_id, identifiers required) and re-checks name/ci_type/source in the controller (400 otherwise). Calls reconciliationEngine.reconcileCI(discoveredCI) and responds 200 with {_success:true,_data:{ci_id,action},_message}. On failure returns 500. Side effect: creates or updates the CI in the CMDB via the reconciliation engine.
issue_note: The created-vs-updated label is derived heuristically with ciId.includes('_') ? 'created' : 'updated' (reconciliation.controller.ts:116), which infers the action from the format of the returned id rather than the engine's actual outcome and can report the wrong action.

user_story: As a data steward, I want to list pending reconciliation conflicts so that I can review and resolve them.
expected_behavior: Reads status (default 'pending', route allows pending/resolved/dismissed), limit (capped at 1000), offset, then queries reconciliation_conflicts WHERE status=$1 ORDER BY created_at DESC with LIMIT/OFFSET, plus a COUNT for pagination. Responds 200 with {_success:true,_data:[{id,ci_id,conflict_type,source_data,target_data,conflicting_fields,status,created_at}],_pagination:{total,count,offset,limit}}. On failure returns 500.

user_story: As a data steward, I want to resolve a conflict by accepting source, accepting target, or merging so that the CI ends up with the correct values.
expected_behavior: Validates resolution against ['accept_source','accept_target','merge'] (400 otherwise). Loads the conflict by id (404 if not found), then UPDATEs reconciliation_conflicts SET status='resolved', resolution_data=JSON({resolution,merged_data}), resolved_at=NOW(). Responds 200 with {_success:true,_data:{conflict_id,resolution,status:'resolved'},_message}. On failure returns 500. Note: it records the chosen resolution but does not itself apply merged_data to the CI record.

user_story: As an administrator, I want to list reconciliation rules and their configuration so that I understand how identity matching and merging are configured.
expected_behavior: Queries reconciliation_rules (id, name, identification_rules, merge_strategies, enabled, created_at, updated_at) ORDER BY created_at DESC. Responds 200 with {_success:true,_data:[rule,...]}. On failure returns 500. No parameters.

user_story: As an administrator, I want to create a reconciliation rule with identification rules and merge strategies so that the engine matches and merges CIs the way I want.
expected_behavior: Validates body via createRuleSchema (name, identification_rules array required; each rule has attribute/priority/match_type/match_confidence) and re-checks name + identification_rules array in the controller (400 otherwise). INSERTs into reconciliation_rules with JSON-stringified rule arrays and created_at/updated_at NOW(), RETURNING *. Responds 201 with {_success:true,_data:{id,name,identification_rules,merge_strategies,enabled,created_at},_message}. On failure returns 500.

user_story: As an administrator, I want to see each source's authority score so that I understand which source wins during merge conflicts.
expected_behavior: Queries source_authority (source_name, authority_score, description) ORDER BY authority_score DESC. Responds 200 with {_success:true,_data:[{source_name,authority_score,description}]}. On failure returns 500. No parameters.

user_story: As an administrator, I want to set or update a source's authority score so that I can tune which source is trusted for conflicting fields.
expected_behavior: Validates authority_score is present and within 1-10 (controller returns 400 otherwise; route schema also enforces integer 1-10 required). Performs an UPSERT into source_authority (INSERT ... ON CONFLICT (source_name) DO UPDATE) keyed by the :source path param. Responds 200 with {_success:true,_data:{source_name,authority_score,description},_message}. On failure returns 500.

user_story: As a data steward, I want to see which sources have contributed data to a CI and when so that I can trust its provenance.
expected_behavior: Queries ci_source_lineage WHERE ci_id=$1 (source_name, source_id, confidence_score, first_seen_at, last_seen_at) ORDER BY last_seen_at DESC. Responds 200 with {_success:true,_data:{ci_id,sources:[...]}}. Returns an empty sources array when no lineage exists. On failure returns 500.

user_story: As a data steward, I want to know which source provided each field value of a CI so that I can audit field-level provenance.
expected_behavior: Queries ci_field_sources WHERE ci_id=$1 (field_name, field_value, source_name, updated_at) ORDER BY field_name. Responds 200 with {_success:true,_data:{ci_id,fields:[...]}}. Returns an empty fields array when nothing recorded. On failure returns 500.

user_story: As an executive, I want a summary of IT spend, cost by capability, service health, risk, and value so that I can make portfolio-level decisions.
expected_behavior: Parses days (default 30) into a TimeRange and calls dashboardService.getExecutiveSummary, which reads active CIs from Neo4j and aggregates TBM/BSM attributes into totalITSpend, costByCapability, serviceHealthByTier, riskMatrix, and topCostDrivers; costTrends and valueScorecard are generated as placeholder/derived data. Responds 200 with {success:true,data,timeRange}. On failure returns 500 with {success:false,error,message}.

user_story: As a CIO, I want service availability, change success, incident response, configuration accuracy, and cost-vs-budget so that I can oversee IT operations.
expected_behavior: Parses days into a TimeRange and calls dashboardService.getCIOMetrics, which pulls active CIs with ITIL/TBM/BSM attributes from Neo4j to compute serviceAvailability, configurationAccuracy, and costByCapability vs budget; changeSuccessRates, incidentResponseTimes, and capacityPlanning are returned as hardcoded mock values. Responds 200 with {success:true,data,timeRange}. On failure returns 500.

user_story: As an ITSM manager, I want open incidents, in-progress changes, CI status breakdown, and SLA compliance so that I can run service management.
expected_behavior: Calls dashboardService.getITSMDashboard (no time range). Derives ciStatus from a Neo4j status breakdown (count plus up to 5 sample CIs per status). openIncidents, changesInProgress, slaCompliance are hardcoded mock data; topFailingCIs and baselineCompliance return empty arrays. Responds 200 with {success:true,data}. On failure returns 500.

user_story: As a FinOps practitioner, I want cloud costs by provider, on-prem vs cloud, cost by tower, budget variance, unit economics, and optimization recommendations so that I can manage spend.
expected_behavior: Parses days into a TimeRange and calls dashboardService.getFinOpsDashboard, which reads active CIs with TBM attributes and provider from Neo4j, then computes cloudCosts, onPremVsCloud, costByTower, budgetVariance, unitEconomics, and costOptimization from those TBM values. Responds 200 with {success:true,data,timeRange}. On failure returns 500.

user_story: As a service owner, I want service health, revenue at risk, customer impact, compliance, value-stream health, and dependencies so that I can manage business services.
expected_behavior: Resolves serviceId from the optional path param or ?serviceId query. Calls dashboardService.getBusinessServiceDashboard: when serviceId is given it matches that single CI by id, otherwise all active CIs, and aggregates serviceHealth plus real serviceDependencies from Neo4j; revenueAtRisk, customerImpact, complianceStatus, and valueStreamHealth are returned as hardcoded mock values. Responds 200 with {success:true,data,serviceId}. On failure returns 500.
